title: disable proc check
text: disable proc check Disable the Sybase connector check for stored procedures. USYS$SYB_PARAMS = disable proc check Description When opening a table, the connector always checks whether that table has stored procedures defined for it. If you know that all tables have stored procedures defined for them, you can use this option to disable the check. This can improve performance because the connector performs one less command during an open table request. When this option is used, stored procedures are used for all tables (except Sybase system tables), unless stored procedures are disabled with the disable procs connector option. Note: When stored procedures are enabled and the procedure check is disabled, procedures must exist for all Uniface entities. Related Links disable procs Disable the use of all stored procedures. Sybase Stored Procedures The SYB connector supports the use of Sybase stored procedures. These can also be activated as service stored procedures. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
